highlights.title: Claude Code CVE-2026-39861: Sandbox Escape via Symlink
highlights.summary: A security vulnerability has been disclosed that allows sandbox escape through symlink manipulation in Claude Code. If you are running Claude Code in any environment, you should update to the latest version immediately to protect your system. Check the GitHub Security Advisory for full details and remediation steps.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to their model lineup. This release is relevant for Claude Code users as newer models typically bring improved coding reasoning and instruction-following capabilities. Check the Anthropic blog for details on what's new and how to access it via Claude Code.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.136: Auto Mode Hard Deny & OTel Survey Support
highlights.summary: Version 2.1.136 adds `settings.autoMode.hard_deny`, allowing teams to set classifier rules that unconditionally block certain actions regardless of user intent — a powerful guardrail for enterprise deployments. It also introduces `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_FEEDBACK_SURVEY_FOR_OTEL` for organizations using OpenTelemetry to re-enable session quality surveys. Update now to take advantage of these new enterprise-grade controls.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.132: Session ID in Bash & Alternate Screen Opt-Out
highlights.summary: This release exposes `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` inside Bash tool subprocesses, making it easier to correlate hook events with shell commands for logging and debugging. You can also now set `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` to keep the conversation in your normal terminal scroll buffer instead of the fullscreen renderer — handy for piping output or screen readers. These quality-of-life improvements are worth picking up if you write custom hooks or work in constrained terminal environments.

highlights.title: Using Claude Code: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of HTML
highlights.summary: This piece explores how Claude Code excels at generating plain HTML solutions for problems developers often over-engineer with complex frameworks. The author provides live examples showing how prompting for simple HTML outputs can be surprisingly powerful and portable. If you find yourself reaching for a heavy stack, try asking Claude Code for an HTML-first approach first.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a focused 30-minute walkthrough of Claude Code's core features, making it ideal for developers who want a fast but thorough introduction. It covers setup, typical workflows, and practical prompting strategies you can apply immediately. Bookmark this if you prefer video learning over documentation.

highlights.title: Complete 60-Minute Claude Code Masterclass (Chinese, with Full Documentation)
highlights.summary: This comprehensive Bilibili tutorial covers everything from installation to advanced Claude Code usage, accompanied by full written documentation — the creator spent three weeks producing it. Chinese-speaking developers will find this one of the most thorough single-video resources available for getting up to speed. Even if you're not a Chinese speaker, the linked documentation may contain useful structured notes.

tips_of_the_day: Always update Claude Code to the latest version before starting a new project — recent releases often patch security vulnerabilities (like CVE-2026-39861) that could expose your local filesystem.

Set `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_ALTERNATE_SCREEN=1` in your shell profile if you work in a terminal multiplexer like tmux or screen; this keeps Claude Code's output in your normal scroll buffer so you never lose context.

Before scaffolding a new feature with a heavy framework, try prompting Claude Code with 'build this as a single HTML file first' — you may be surprised how often the simple version is all you actually need.
